recommended_next_actions: Review the new imported provider-evidence proof from April 3, 2026 and continue from `capture_second_node_node_local_proof` rather than reopening the single-node route, session, Java-cluster, architecture, failure, support-pain, config, configuration-pattern, provider-evidence, or second-node-shape slices.

The successor now has all three current-node boundary pieces in place: a target-backed `distributed_and_external_knowledge_layers` packet, a bounded `provider_specific_integration_evidence_current_node` packet, and a reusable second-node request plus thin cross-node envelope contract.

The strongest next ambiguity is no longer whether AWS and Azure have bounded current-node evidence. It is what a second imported node-local proof changes, preserves, or disproves across the same successor artifact shape.

Use the reusable second-node request and wrapper templates instead of inventing a one-off second-node workflow, and keep the cross-node envelope inactive until a real second imported node proof exists.
Keep using the remote-owned runtime flow: adf-dev at 10.167.2.151 remains the runtime and support-facing host, while 10.167.2.150 remains the runtime evidence source.

Treat the current lab posture as exploration-friendly: bounded misconfiguration or temporary breakage on 10.167.2.150 is acceptable because the lab is quickly redeployable and later distributed-architecture labs are expected.

If promotion-compatible remote proof later matters for this pack, record and address the missed fresh-pack rehearsal step separately rather than pretending the current long-lived pack already has that certification.

required_gates.summary: Confirm the fresh successor template remains PackFactory-valid and can materialize a first proving-ground build-pack without broadening the testing surface.
